title: A broad-spectrum lasso peptide antibiotic targeting the bacterial ribosome
summary: Discovery, characterization, and mechanism of action of lariocidin, a novel ribosome-targeting lasso peptide antibiotic

discovery_results.compound_class: First lasso peptide shown to inhibit bacterial ribosome

spectrum_of_activity.significance: Highly significant for Gram-negative bacteria resistant to last-resort antibiotics

mechanism_of_action.binding_site_novelty: Unique ribosomal site distinct from well-characterized sites

lariocidin.structure: Lasso peptide with C-terminal tail threaded through macrolactam ring

lariocidin_b.feature: Double isopeptide bond creating double-lariat structure
lariocidin_b.note: First lasso peptide with two isopeptide bonds

cellular_uptake_studies.fluorescent_labeling: Successfully labeled LAR with fluorescent marker
cellular_uptake_studies.cell_accumulation: Fluorescently-labeled LAR accumulated in cytoplasm of E. coli cells

spontaneous_resistant_mutants.methodology: Plating on medium supplemented with LAR

genomic_analysis.initial_mutations: Genes related to electron transport chain and respiration

mutation_interpretation.finding: Deletion of individual identified genes showed no significant MIC changes
mutation_interpretation.conclusion: Aerobic respiration alterations affect LAR uptake but are not direct targets
mutation_interpretation.supporting_evidence: LAR showed 4-8 fold MIC increase under anaerobic conditions

ribosomal_target_confirmation.challenge: Redundant rRNA genes prevent direct target site mutation recovery
ribosomal_target_confirmation.method: Used E. coli SQ110DTC with single rRNA operon

minimum_inhibitory_concentrations.interpretation: Uptake mechanism depends on aerobic metabolism but target is ribosome

resistance_development_potential.mechanism_novelty: Novel ribosomal site and mode of action
resistance_development_potential.resistance_advantage: Unaffected by common resistance mechanisms to other antibiotics

in_vivo_efficacy.model: Mouse model of Acinetobacter baumannii infection
in_vivo_efficacy.clinical_relevance: Demonstrates therapeutic potential in living organism

ribosomal_binding_site.mechanism: Interferes with translation elongation by inhibiting translocation
ribosomal_binding_site.secondary_effect: Induces miscoding errors in protein synthesis

clinical_significance.gap_addressed: No ribosome-targeting lasso peptides previously reported
clinical_significance.mechanism_advantage: Novel ribosomal site avoids common resistance mechanisms
clinical_significance.resistance_cross_protection: Unaffected by resistance to conventional translation inhibitors
clinical_significance.therapeutic_opportunity: New chemical scaffold for antibacterial drug development

antimicrobial_landscape.lariocidin_advantage: Novel binding site and mechanism escape these resistance pathways

research_outcomes.structure_activity: Lasso structure provides unique ribosomal binding properties
research_outcomes.chemical_scaffold: Lasso peptide scaffold for alternative antibiotics

future_therapeutic_directions.drug_development: Foundation for new ribosomal inhibitor drugs
future_therapeutic_directions.structural_optimization: Potential for chemical derivatization and improvement
future_therapeutic_directions.combination_therapy: Potential synergy with conventional antibiotics targeting other sites
